diagrams.title: Modular Program Structure
diagrams.description: Shows how a C program is organized into separate modules: header files for declarations, source files for implementations, and main program that integrates them
elements: Header Files (.h) - Function declarations, macros, type definitions
Main Program - Calls functions from modules
Compiler - Compiles each source file separately
Linker - Combines object files into executable
diagrams.purpose: Understanding multi-file modular program organization and compilation process

diagrams.title: Function Call Flow
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the flow of control when a function is called: from main to sub-function and back, showing parameter passing and return value
elements: Calling Function (caller) - Contains function call with arguments
Parameter Passing - Arguments copied/passed by address
Called Function - Executes with parameters, performs task
Stack Frame - Shows how each call maintains its own local variables
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how functions interact and how data flows between modules

diagrams.title: Recursion Stack Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how recursive calls build up a stack and then unwind when base case is reached
elements: Initial Call - First invocation of recursive function
Recursive Calls - Stack of calls building up
Unwinding - Returns propagate back through call stack
Result - Final value returned to original caller
diagrams.purpose: Understanding recursion mechanism and importance of base case in preventing infinite recursion

diagrams.title: Pass-by-Value vs Pass-by-Reference
diagrams.description: Compares two parameter passing techniques showing how data flows differently in each case
elements: Pass-by-Value: Original variable, Copy created, Changes to copy don't affect original
Pass-by-Reference: Original variable, Address passed, Changes to pointed location affect original
Visual comparison of memory locations and modifications
diagrams.purpose: Clarifying difference between parameter passing techniques and when to use each
